title_en: Contracts and Judicial Procedure in the Hashiyat al-Dasuqi
content_en:
Al-Dasuqi's hashiyah on the sections of Al-Sharh al-Kabir dealing with mu'amalat (transactions) and qada' (judicial procedure) reflects the Maliki school's sophisticated approach to commercial and civil law, shaped by centuries of application in the diverse societies of North Africa, Egypt, and West Africa. In the law of sales, al-Dasuqi engages with the conditions for a valid contract with characteristic analytical depth. The Maliki school requires that the subject of a sale be pure (tahir), useful (muntafi' bihi), deliverable (maqdur ala taslimih), owned by the seller, and free from excessive uncertainty (gharar). Al-Dasuqi's hashiyah clarifies each of these conditions, noting where the Maliki school's interpretation differs from other schools and the evidential basis for Maliki positions. The Maliki school's treatment of bay' al-'inah (a form of two-contract transaction sometimes used to circumvent the riba prohibition) is addressed in the hashiyah with the school's generally prohibitive stance. Al-Dasuqi explains why the Maliki school prohibits this arrangement even when the Hanafi and some other schools may permit variants of it under certain conditions, rooting the prohibition in the Maliki principle of sadd adh-dhara'i — blocking the means to harm even when the immediate act appears technically permissible.

The Maliki school's distinctive approach to the option of inspection (khiyar al-ru'yah) — the buyer's right to return goods he has not yet seen at the time of purchase — is addressed in the hashiyah. The Maliki school in principle permits the buyer to cancel the contract upon inspection if the goods do not match his reasonable expectation, and al-Dasuqi discusses the conditions for this right and when it lapses. In the area of agency (wakalah), al-Dasuqi elaborates the Maliki conditions for a valid agency contract, the scope of the agent's authority, and the agent's liability for goods entrusted to him. The Maliki school generally treats the agent as a trustee (amin) rather than a guarantor — meaning he is liable only for loss caused by his negligence, not for loss from factors beyond his control. The sections on judicial procedure (qada') in Al-Sharh al-Kabir, elaborated by al-Dasuqi, address the qualifications of judges, the rules of evidence, and the procedures for resolving disputes. The Maliki school requires two male witnesses of upright character ('adl) for most legal matters, with women's testimony counted as half of a man's in financial matters per the Quranic guidance. Al-Dasuqi discusses the conditions for witness reliability, what disqualifies a witness, and how the court should handle conflicting testimony. The law of oaths (ayman) — including the conditions under which oaths are binding, the expiation (kaffara) for breaking an oath, and the use of oaths in judicial settings — is addressed systematically. The Maliki school's position on the oath with righteous intention (niyya) that does not violate the apparent meaning of the oath is nuanced and al-Dasuqi's hashiyah provides valuable clarification. Al-Dasuqi's Hashiyah stands as the pinnacle of classical Maliki scholarship in Egypt and North Africa. Its combination of technical precision, independent analysis, and comprehensive coverage makes it an indispensable reference not only for historical understanding of the Maliki tradition but for contemporary Maliki legal practice across the regions where the school remains dominant."
content_id:
Hasyiyah al-Dasuqi atas bagian-bagian Asy-Syarh al-Kabir yang membahas mu'amalat (transaksi) dan qadha (prosedur peradilan) mencerminkan pendekatan madzhab Maliki yang canggih terhadap hukum komersial dan keadilan. Al-Dasuqi menyediakan analisis yang sangat rinci tentang berbagai kontrak dalam fikih Maliki — jual beli, sewa, kemitraan, dan pembiayaan perdagangan — dengan perhatian khusus pada pertanyaan-pertanyaan di mana madzhab Maliki mengambil posisi yang berbeda dari madzhab-madzhab lain.

Bagian-bagian larangan riba dan gharar (ketidakpastian) dalam hasyiyah ini merupakan salah satu yang paling rinci dalam literatur Maliki. Al-Dasuqi bekerja melalui pertanyaan-pertanyaan tentang kapan ketidakpastian dalam kontrak mencapai tingkat yang melarang kontrak tersebut, kapan pengaturan kontrak yang tampak menjadi sarana riba, dan bagaimana membedakan transaksi yang diizinkan dari yang dilarang dalam situasi-situasi ambigu. Analisis-analisis ini sangat relevan bagi hukum keuangan Islam kontemporer.

Bagian-bagian prosedur peradilan mencakup standar-standar yang diperlukan untuk kesaksian, interpretasi kontrak, dan penyelesaian sengketa dalam fikih Maliki. Al-Dasuqi menyajikan pandangan-pandangan madzhab tentang masalah-masalah seperti persyaratan bukti, kriteria untuk kesaksian yang dapat diterima, dan cara menangani konflik antara klaim-klaim yang bersaing dengan cara yang menunjukkan kepraktisan dan kekomprehensifan madzhab Maliki dalam yurisprudensi hukum.
title_id: Kontrak dan Prosedur Peradilan dalam Hasyiyat al-Dasuqi
